## What does this PR do?

<!-- One or two sentences. Link the issue it closes: "Closes #123". -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (no consensus change)
- [ ] Feature / improvement (no consensus change)
- [ ] Consensus / protocol / economic change — **requires a linked TIP** (link it below; this implementation cannot merge or activate until the TIP's acceptance criteria are met)
- [ ] Docs / tooling / tests only

## TIP (consensus-affecting changes only)

<!-- Link the TIP. A consensus change requires a linked TIP, but the TIP does not
     need to be Active yet — this PR may BE the reference implementation. It just
     cannot merge/activate until the TIP's acceptance criteria (tips/README.md) are met. -->
TIP: N/A

## Checklist

- [ ] Focused on a single logical change
- [ ] Tests added/updated and passing locally
- [ ] CI is expected to be green (build + test)
- [ ] Commits are signed off (`git commit -s`); maintainers also GPG-sign
- [ ] No force-push expected during review; no unrelated reformatting
- [ ] If this bumps a submodule pointer, the new commit is on a protected ref of that submodule

## How was this tested?

<!-- Commands, environment, results. For consensus changes include test vectors. -->
